name: KB B-Stance Flow (@Asgooch)
description: Unilateral kettlebell flow: B-stance swing → swing clean → B-stance squat → split stance snatch → OH lunge. 5 reps each side, 5 rounds. Don't put the bell down. Video: https://x.com/Asgooch/status/2026011472489324584

sets.name: B-Stance Flow

exercises.notes: B-stance: one foot back, heel up. Swing between legs, explosive hip drive. Bell swings up to about chest height.
→ On the next swing, CATCH it directly into rack position at your shoulder. Don't dead stop — ride the momentum into the clean.
→ Bell is in rack. Stay in B-stance (back foot heel still up). Squat down and stand back up. Bell stays at shoulder.
→ From rack, punch the bell straight overhead in one motion. Lock out arm, stand tall. Split stance snatch — you're still in B-stance when it goes up.
→ Bell locked out overhead, step forward into a lunge. Stand straight up from the lunge with arm STILL locked overhead. That's 1 rep of the full flow.

sets.notes: 5 reps each side × 5 rounds. Don't put the bell down between moves — one continuous chain. Each rep flows: swing → catch into rack → squat → punch overhead → lunge → stand up with arm locked.

tips: All same arm, don't put the bell down until you switch sides.
The swing clean is catching a full swing into rack — control the momentum.
Split stance snatch: punch the bell overhead from rack while still in B-stance.
OH lunge: stand straight up with arm STILL locked overhead. That's the hard part.
@Asgooch used a 24kg (53lb) bell. Go lighter to learn the transitions.
